creator: simonmar@microsoft.com
text:
[ sorry for the delay, one of our mail servers has a huge backlog here,
sigh. ]

> On Fri, Jul 14, 2000 at 11:39:10PM +0100, Simon Marlow wrote:
> > >Synopsis:       Update ports/lang/ghc to version 4.08
> > Remove patches dir; the patches have been incorporated into the main
> > sources.
> 
> I get errors compiling this..:
> 
> ../../driver/ghc-inplace -recomp -cpp -fglasgow-exts -fvia-C
> -Rghc-timing -O -split-objs -H32m -package-name std -static 
> -hisuf p_hi
> -split-objs -prof -H8m  -c PrelArr.lhs -o PrelArr.p_o -osuf p_o
> <<ghc: 374690752 bytes, 19 GCs, 3337488/7630928 avg/max bytes 
> residency
> (5 samples), 32M in use, 0.00 INIT (0.01 elapsed), 13.04 MUT (13.97
> elapsed), 3.49 GC (3.64 elapsed) :ghc>>
> Couldn't grok consistency: ghc_cc_ID:
> .byte 0x40,0x28,0x23,0x29,0x63,0x63,0x20,0x50,0x72,0x65
> .byte 0x6c,0x41,0x72,0x72,0x2e,0x6c,0x68,0x73,0x9,0x33
> .byte 0x36,0x2e,0x31,0x2c,0x5f,0x70,0x2c,0x0
> Couldn't grok consistency: ghc_hsc_ID:
> .byte 0x40,0x28,0x23,0x29,0x68,0x73,0x63,0x20,0x50,0x72
> .byte 0x65,0x6c,0x41,0x72,0x72,0x2e,0x6c,0x68,0x73,0x9
> .byte 0x34,0x30,0x2e,0x30,0x2c,0x5f,0x70,0x2c,0x0

These should be harmless.  I don't remember seeing them on my box, but I'll
look into it.

> /: write failed, file system is full
> 
> /: write failed, file system is full
> 
> /: write failed, file system is full
> 
> /: write failed, file system is full
> 
> /: write failed, file system is full
> 
> /: write failed, file system is full
> ghc-inplace:Failed writing /tmp/ghc8726__166.s
> gmake[2]: *** [PrelArr.p_o] Error 1

Hmmm.  You need a fairly large /tmp to compile this beast.  Alternatively,
you can set TMPDIR to somewhere with more space.

In the interim, you might want to change the line in files/build.mk

	GhcLibWays=p

to
	GhcLibWays=

which will stop it building the profiling libraries.  This should halve the
build time.  Maybe I can separate out the profiling libraries into another
package - what do you think?

> Even though:
> <2 5226-0> (00-07-15 17:03:49) [will@argon /usr/ports/lang/ghc]% df
> Filesystem  1K-blocks     Used    Avail Capacity  Mounted on
> /dev/ad0s2a     49583    36070     9547    79%    /

Yes, 10M probably isn't enough.

> /dev/ad0s2f   2924462  2095524   594982    78%    /usr
> /dev/ad0s2e     49583    12196    33421    27%    /var
> procfs              4        4        0   100%    /proc
> /dev/ad0s5     974976   228064   746912    23%    /ext
> /dev/ad0s1    2093856  1469472   624384    70%    /win
> 
> Then when I try to remake the compile to observe it actually trying to
> take up my HD space:
> 
> --------------------------------------------------------------
> ----------
> ===fptools== Recursively making `all' for ways: p ...
> PWD = /usr/ports/lang/ghc/work/fptools/ghc/lib/std
> --------------------------------------------------------------
> ----------
> --------------------------------------------------------------
> ----------
> ==fptools== gmake way=p all;
> PWD = /usr/ports/lang/ghc/work/fptools/ghc/lib/std
> --------------------------------------------------------------
> ----------
> gmake[4]: *** No rule to make target `Array.p_o', needed by
> `libHSstd_p.a'.  Stop.
> gmake[3]: *** [all] Error 1
> gmake[2]: *** [all] Error 1
> gmake[1]: *** [all] Error 1
> gmake: *** [all] Error 1
> *** Error code 2
> 
> :-(

It looks like re-making after a failure isn't working.  This is due to the
weirdness in the package Makefile to do with bootstrapping the compiler from
C sources, I'm not sure I can fix this but I'll take a look.

Cheers,
	Simon
